description_long: This sculpture depicts a classical female figure, reminiscent of ancient Greek or Roman art, with a draped lower body and an exposed upper torso. The figure is missing both arms and stands in a contrapposto pose, with the weight shifted onto one leg, creating a naturalistic curve in the body. The head is slightly turned to the left, and the facial features are delicately rendered, with wavy hair styled back. The drapery of the garment is intricately detailed, cascading in folds around the hips and legs, while the upper body remains bare. The surface appears to be made of stone or marble, with a smooth, matte finish and subtle weathering or patina in some areas. The overall form is elegant and lifelike, evoking the timeless beauty of classical statuary.
description: A classical-style female statue with a draped lower body, missing arms, and finely detailed hair and facial features. The sculpture has a smooth, stone-like surface and stands in a naturalistic pose.
description_view_0: Front view showing the exposed torso, draped lower body, and detailed facial features; both arms are missing.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the face, the curve of the body, and the flowing drapery.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back, the gathered drapery, and the hair styled in a bun.
description_view_3: Three-quarter rear view emphasizing the curvature of the back and the intricate folds of the garment.
attribute_location_context: No specific setting or environment is visible; the background is isolated and does not provide contextual clues.
